title: Quip.com Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and tips for using Quip.com effectively.

commands.description: Log into your Quip account via CLI.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to authenticate your CLI session with Quip.

commands.description: List all documents in your Quip account.
commands.scenario: Use to view all your documents quickly from the command line.

commands.description: Create a new document with a specified title.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to create a new document programmatically.

commands.description: Delete a document by its ID.
commands.scenario: Use to remove a document you no longer need.

commands.description: Export a document to PDF format.
commands.scenario: Use when you need a PDF version of your document.

commands.description: Share a document with another user via email.
commands.scenario: Use to collaborate by sharing documents with team members.

commands.description: Update the content of a document by its ID.
commands.scenario: Use to modify the content of an existing document.

commands.description: List all folders in your Quip account.
commands.scenario: Use to view the folder structure of your documents.

commands.description: Move a document to a different folder.
commands.scenario: Use to organize documents into folders.

commands.description: List all users in your Quip account.
commands.scenario: Use to view all users who have access to your Quip account.

commands.description: Create a new folder with a specified name.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to organize documents into a new folder.

commands.description: Rename an existing folder by its ID.
commands.scenario: Use to change the name of a folder for better organization.
